item.title: Scaling graph foundation models with pretraining on heterogeneous structures
item.dc:creator: Sidharth Sinha, Anson Bastos, Xuchao Zhang
item.description: Researchers at Microsoft Research propose a new framework for pretraining graph foundation models on heterogeneous structures.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers at Microsoft Research propose a new framework for pretraining graph foundation models on heterogeneous structures, demonstrating significant gains across diverse downstream tasks. The framework, described in a forthcoming paper, leverages a unified token vocabulary that bridges molecular graphs, citation networks, and knowledge graphs.

The team trained on a curated corpus comprising several billion edges and evaluated transfer performance on link prediction, node classification, and graph-level regression benchmarks. Results indicate consistent improvements over single-domain baselines, with the largest gains observed on small-data downstream tasks where pretraining matters most.

The paper, currently under peer review, will be released alongside open-source code and pretrained checkpoints upon acceptance. Future work will investigate scaling laws specific to graph data and the interaction between graph topology and token vocabulary size.

Opens in a new tabThe post Scaling graph foundation models with pretraining on heterogeneous structures appeared first on Microsoft Research.

item.title: Short post
item.dc:creator: Brenda Potts
item.content:encoded:
tiny

Opens in a new tabThe post Short post appeared first on Microsoft Research.
